verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN51) is a type I integral membrane protein that resides primarily in the trans-Golgi network and cycles between early endosomes and the TGN. Its topology places the luminal/extracellular domain facing the TGN lumen (not the extracellular space), and its cytoplasmic tail contains the sorting signals that drive TGN retention/retrieval. It does not reach the plasma membrane outer leaflet as a canonical step in its lifecycle. Pre-no checklist: no cell-surface-directed clinical program known; not a shed ectodomain precursor reaching the PM surface; no documented wash-resistant TM-partner tethering at PM; no documented minority PM pool from lysosomal exocytosis; no cancer ecto-pool or cell-death surface exposure reported; no activation-induced PM display; no CD designation or surface-protein gene-group membership; NCBI summary is unambiguous for endomembrane residency. Passing through early endosomes during recycling does not constitute PM outer-leaflet access.
